heroHeadline: Post-Anniversary J&K: Civil Defence Drills in Anantnag and Ganderbal; Nearly 10 Lakh Tourists Visited Pahalgam Since Baisaran Attack; Mughal Road Reopens
heroSubtitle: One day after the first anniversary of the Pahalgam massacre, J&K authorities conducted civil defence blackout drills in Anantnag and Ganderbal districts — testing air raid and emergency protocols in communities still shaped by the May 2025 Operation Sindoor shelling. Official data released April 23 showed nearly 993,596 tourists visited Pahalgam in the year since the April 22, 2025 Baisaran attack, demonstrating tourism resilience even as the meadow massacre site remains sealed. The Mughal Road connecting Shopian to Poonch reopened for limited daytime traffic. Diplomatic tensions persist: Pakistan minister Attaullah Tarar's 'false pride, hollow mindset' tirade on April 22 drew Indian condemnation, while the Semafor-documented 'India bristles as Pakistan has its diplomatic moment' dynamic continues to define regional strategic competition.
footerNote: Data sourced from Indian MEA/MOD, Pakistan ISPR/MFA, UN Security Council, SIPRI, Crisis Group, HRW, Stimson Center
